source.title: Role of the lateral prefrontal cortex in executive behavioral control

confidence_rationale: Physiological Reviews synthesis from the Tanji lab focused on lateral PFC, pre-SMA, SMA, and premotor cortex contributions to executive sequence control. Provides the premotor/SMA-analog substrate boundary for REE — important for deciding which functions belong inside the lateral-PFC analogue vs in adjacent premotor/SMA substrates. Slightly older (2008) but canonical.
failure_signatures: If pre-SMA and lateral PFC neurons showed identical functional properties for sequence ordering and rule holding, the distinction REE relies on (lateral-PFC for rules, premotor/SMA for sequence execution) would collapse.
If SMA lesions produced selective rule-memory deficits rather than sequence-execution deficits, the substrate boundaries would need redrawing.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Lateral PFC and adjacent premotor/SMA regions form a functionally graded executive-control system. Lateral PFC handles rule selection and goal maintenance; pre-SMA handles sequence planning and set-switching; SMA and dorsal premotor cortex handle sequence execution and stimulus-response mapping. These regions are interconnected but functionally separable.
mapping.ree_translation: Gives REE the substrate boundary it needs between the lateral-PFC-analogue (rules, goals) and the premotor/SMA-analogue (committed sequences, stimulus-action bindings). In REE terms, existing E3 sequence-selection machinery most closely maps to premotor/SMA-analogue function; the lateral-PFC analogue is upstream, setting the rule context that E3 operates within. MECH-261 write gating differs between these: rule writes during external_task + internal_planning, sequence-execution writes primarily during external_task.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The 2008 review predates much of the pre-SMA/SMA boundary literature that has since been refined. It also relies on primate single-unit work that doesn't perfectly translate to rodent premotor organisation. REE should treat the broad lateral-PFC vs premotor/SMA distinction as robust but not over-commit to the 2008 paper's specific anatomical parcels.
mapping.source_context: Primate single-unit recording, lesion, and microstimulation work on rule/sequence tasks.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high (Physiological Reviews, Tanji lab). Mapping fidelity moderate-strong: the distinction between rule-holding and sequence-execution substrates is exactly what REE needs. Transfer risk moderate: the specific anatomical parcels used in 2008 have been refined since, and rodent-primate mapping is imperfect for premotor/SMA structures.
